objective: Implement the public-reference bundle tooling: manifest/source-record/quality-score/acquisition-task models, deterministic source scoring and A/B/C tiering, the optcg-reference CLI, and the reference-image normalization pipeline (corner detection, rectification, registration to a clean English reference, sleeve/slab interference flagging, conservative exposure/WB metadata, weak-alignment rejection).
current_state: Schemas frozen by two-lane-p0-state-machine (docs/agent-ops/reference-bundle.schema.json, reference-source-quality.schema.json, acquisition-task.schema.json). geometry.py provides corner detection / rectification / residual registration for the physical lane; no bundle tooling exists.
assumptions: geometry.py public API (corner detection, warp, residual registration) is imported read-only and is sufficient; if it is not, stop and report instead of editing it.
Bundle roots live under ~/GenkiStuff/optcg-reference-lab/public-reference-bundles/ and are never committed; tests use synthetic fixtures in temp dirs only.
acceptance_criteria: Manifest, source-record, quality-score, tier, and acquisition-task models round-trip and validate against the frozen schemas.
Scoring is deterministic with documented weights; tiering yields A/B/C; only Tier A or human-reviewed Tier B set eligible_for_profile true.
Blocked retrieval creates an acquisition-task record retaining the URL; no code path automates around anti-bot controls (no header spoofing, no scraping fallbacks).

Normalization preserves original file hashes, writes normalized outputs alongside originals, flags sleeve/slab/toploader interference regions, records conservative exposure/white-balance metadata, and rejects weak alignment with a diagnostic instead of accepting it.
No global normalization step that removes real per-source foil appearance differences.
Every accepted media file is hash-recorded (blake3) in the manifest.
required_tests: uv run ruff check src tests
stop_conditions: geometry.py public API is insufficient for reference-image rectification.
Any requirement would need edits outside allowed_paths.
A test would require real marketplace imagery (synthetic fixtures only).
expected_outputs: reference_bundle.py + reference_bundle_cli.py (optcg-reference: init-bundle, add-source, add-media, acquisition-task, score, tier, validate)
